title: 'Jaishankar spoke to Iran FM 3 times': India flags ships safety, energy security concerns
content: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar held three recent talks with his Iranian counterpart, focusing on maritime shipping safety and India's energy security amid rising Middle East tensions. Iran has ensured safe passage for Indian-flagged vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial route for global oil trade and India's energy imports.

title: Liberia-flagged tanker ‘Shenlong’ carrying Saudi crude enters India via Strait of Hormuz
content: A Liberian-flagged oil tanker, captained by an Indian, successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz to reach Mumbai Port. This marks a significant transit amid heightened Middle East tensions, with Iran reportedly granting safe passage to Indian vessels. Authorities are actively monitoring 28 Indian-flagged ships and seafarers in the Persian Gulf.

title: Why time is ripe for Suryakumar Yadav to follow in Rohit Sharma's footsteps
content: Suryakumar Yadav may have led India to T20 World Cup glory, but his leadership journey still draws heavily from Rohit Sharma’s blueprint. From aggressive cricket to backing players, Rohit's influence remains clear. Now, Surya faces the same defining choice his predecessor once made: step aside at the right time and secure a legacy not just as a champion, but as a visionary leader.

title: Meta temporarily closes Israel office; offers support to staff
content: Meta has temporarily closed its Tel Aviv office and offered hotel stays to employees without bomb shelters amid escalating Middle East conflict. Other tech giants like Nvidia, Amazon, and Snap have also implemented remote work policies. The conflict has impacted regional tech infrastructure, with AWS facilities reportedly hit by drone strikes.

title: T20 World Cup: South Africa, West Indies finally leave India after travel chaos
content: South African and West Indies cricket contingents have finally left India after days of travel chaos. Airspace closures in West Asia disrupted flights, stranding players in Kolkata post-T20 World Cup. The ICC confirmed the safe departure of the remaining 29 South Africans and 16 West Indians, concluding a complex logistical operation amidst challenging global travel conditions.

title: UP teen dies after mother, brother force her to consume poison over interfaith relationship
content: Ghazipur police have arrested a mother and son for allegedly killing the 19-year-old daughter. The duo reportedly acted out of anger over her relationship with a Hindu man. They are accused of forcing her to consume poison. The body was found in a field. The mother and son were apprehended while attempting to flee.

title: ‘He was very angry’: Surya reveals he apologised to Axar after T20 WC snub
content: India captain Suryakumar Yadav shared that Axar Patel was upset after being excluded from the playing XI during India's successful T20 World Cup campaign. Yadav apologised, calling it a team decision. The loss to South Africa became a turning point, prompting the team to treat all subsequent matches as knockouts. India then won their remaining games to secure the trophy.

title: India prepares to add new incentives under PLI scheme, likely to boost Apple & Samsung
content: India's flagship smartphone manufacturing incentive program is undergoing a major overhaul, now linking government subsidies to exports and the use of locally produced components. This revamped PLI scheme aims to move beyond "screwdriver assembly" and will likely benefit major players like Apple and Samsung, while also pushing Chinese brands to increase exports.

title: FCC chairman responds to Amazon’s complaint letter against Elon Musk’s Spacex
content: Amazon has filed a complaint with the FCC against SpaceX's ambitious 1 million-satellite proposal, questioning its feasibility and completeness. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr publicly criticized Amazon's petition, urging them to focus on their own satellite deployment challenges rather than opposing SpaceX's rapid launches.

title: Farooq Abdullah's 1st reaction after assassination attempt: 'Killer always has an advantage'
content: Former Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Farooq Abdullah narrowly escaped an assassination attempt at a wedding. A man fired shots at him, but his security team intervened, saving his life. Abdullah expressed gratitude to God and called for an investigation into the incident. He also commented on the prevailing atmosphere of hatred and the need for unity.

title: Reliance-backed oil refinery in US: What makes Trump’s ‘historic $300bn deal’ significant?
content: The proposed facility in Texas will be Reliance Industries’ second greenfield investment outside India. Reliance Industries, which has a market capitalisation of ₹18.82 lakh crore ($204 billion), runs the world’s largest integrated refinery complex at a single site in Jamnagar, Gujarat.

title: From 100 in 2013 to 1,900 now: How China's growing spy satellite network alarms US
content: A top US Space Force general warned that America must develop offensive capabilities in space to counter China's rapidly growing satellite network.  He emphasized that simply defending satellites won't suffice against potential threats.  The US is enhancing its ability to track and target, acknowledging space as a critical warfighting domain amidst rising global competition.

title: ‘Definitely a medal prospect’: Ex-CWG medallist backs Lakshya for LA 2028 medal
content: India's Lakshya Sen is considered a strong medal contender for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics after his Paris 2024 debut and a strong showing at the 2026 All England Championships. Former player Trupti Murgunde believes Sen is well-positioned but stresses the importance of sustained form, fitness, and wise tournament choices over the next two years.

title: Wedding halted in UP after 2 baraats reach minor bride’s house
content: A wedding in Muzaffarnagar took a bizarre turn when two grooms arrived with separate baraats, each claiming the bride. The ceremony was halted by officials and police after a child helpline alert about the bride's age. Families couldn't produce age proof, leading to the girl being taken to a one-stop centre for verification.

title: 'Mango man of India' who nurtures Modi, Aishwarya and 348+ other varieties of mango
content: Kalimullah Khan, the 'Mango Man of India', has cultivated a remarkable 125-year-old tree bearing over 350 distinct mango varieties. This Padma Shri awardee, who left school early, mastered grafting to create unique hybrids, even naming some after prominent figures. His dedication showcases a blend of traditional knowledge and innovative horticultural skill.

title: Kerosene makes a comeback? Oil firms plan backup as LPG shortage fears rise
content: Authorities are exploring a return of kerosene for kitchens in Uttar Pradesh. This is a precautionary step for a possible LPG shortage in the coming days. Oil companies are assessing if old kerosene distribution networks can be reactivated. The move is a backup plan for emergencies, not a policy change. The focus remains on ensuring cooking fuel access for households.

title: ‘No better feeling’: Bumrah reveals what makes him happiest on the field
content: India pace spearhead Jasprit Bumrah expressed immense joy in delivering under pressure, a mindset that fueled India's historic 2026 T20 World Cup triumph. His match-winning spell in the final, coupled with family presence and a sense of redemption at the Ahmedabad venue, made the victory deeply emotional and fulfilling for the spearhead.

title: Not just oil and gas: How the Iran war is disrupting six other key global commodities
content: Escalating conflict in the Middle East has disrupted global commodity markets beyond oil and gas, impacting aluminium, fertilisers, ethanol, and helium. Tanker movement through the Strait of Hormuz has slowed, with significant implications for essential goods and food exports.  Authorities are working to ensure food supply stability amidst rising logistics costs.

title: 'Rasikan' Hari Murali passes away at 27
content: The passing of 27-year-old Hari Murali has cast a shadow over the Malayalam cinema community. Discovered in his Payyannur residence, Murali, who first captured hearts as a child actor, is remembered not only for his film successes but also for his pursuit of graphic design after stepping away from the spotlight.

title: US 'unfair trade' probe on India, China and 14 others: What Washington is looking into
content: Following a Supreme Court setback on tariffs, the Trump administration has launched new "Section 301" investigations into alleged unfair trade practices by 16 key partners, including China and India. These probes aim to restore tariff pressure and could lead to new import taxes by summer, with officials emphasizing a commitment to protecting US manufacturing and reducing trade deficits.

title: US mainland under threat? What FBI memo says about possible Iranian drone strike
content: An FBI memo suggests Iran might launch a surprise drone attack on California from a ship. This possibility emerged after US strikes on Iran. California officials state they are prepared for emergencies. The alert follows recent US-Iran military actions in the Middle East, which began last month with strikes that killed Iran’s Supreme Leader.

title: 'Not a perfect woman genetically’: Why Telangana HC allowed surrogacy for woman with CAIS condition
content: The Telangana High Court ruled that a married couple with Complete Androgen Insensitivity Syndrome (CAIS) is eligible for surrogacy. The court set aside the State Authority's rejection, emphasizing the law's humanitarian purpose to assist infertile couples and respecting medical certifications.

title: ‘Daughter’s wedding tomorrow, need money’: Farmer breaks down as dispute halts crop auction at mandi
content: A farmer's plea for his daughter's wedding funds turned desperate as crop auctions halted in Guna's Nanakhedi mandi. A dispute over a market inspector's alleged intoxication and price discrepancy led to traders boycotting, leaving farmers stranded and causing highway disruption. The inspector has been removed pending inquiry.

title: Rachel Crosby reacts after Maxx Crosby–Ravens trade drama collapses
content: Trade drama surrounding Maxx Crosby took a sudden turn when the Baltimore Ravens backed out of a deal that would have sent the Raiders star to Baltimore. After medical concerns reportedly halted the trade, Crosby’s agent defended his recovery timeline, insisting the defender remains ahead of schedule. Amid the uncertainty, Rachel Crosby voiced her support with a brief but firm social media reaction. For now, Crosby remains in Las Vegas while his future in the NFL continues to draw attention.

title: San Francisco Giants eye $38.5m Red Sox star in MLB trade rumors
content: San Francisco Giants are eyeing veteran pitcher Lucas Giolito to bolster their rotation ahead of the 2026 season. With several pitchers facing injury concerns, Giolito offers much-needed experience. A potential two-year deal around $35 million could see him slot in as the third starter, benefiting from Oracle Park's pitcher-friendly environment.

title: Cardi B opens up after Stefon Diggs accused of cheating with multiple women
content: Stefon Diggs, the New England Patriots’ star player, has had a tough few weeks after Cardi B ended things with him after he was accused of repeatedly cheating on her. Amid all the mess, the popular Grammy winner Cardi B's painful statements have resurfaced. The statements reveal how Cardi B has dealt with her pain and betrayal and what helped her heal.

title: ‘VVS and I were not big talkers in the middle’: Dravid recalls 2001 Eden Test
content: Rahul Dravid reflects on the iconic Eden Gardens Test victory against Australia, highlighting the tactical brilliance of batting at No. 6 and his marathon partnership with VVS Laxman. He emphasises how this monumental win, a testament to team effort and resilience, proved a significant turning point for Indian cricket, instilling belief and paving the way for future successes.

title: No dosa for now: Infosys trims food court menus across Bengaluru, Pune, Chennai campuses
content: Infosys employees in Bengaluru, Pune, and Chennai face menu limitations and suspended live counters due to a commercial LPG shortage. Food courts are offering reduced options, sourcing some meals externally, and increasing use of electrical appliances and biofuel. The company urges employees to bring food from home to manage the disruption.

title: Iran war enters day 13: Missiles fly, tankers burn, oil prices soar; what’s happening in Middle East?
content: A coordinated US-Israel strike on Iran has ignited a full-blown regional crisis.  Following Ayatollah Khamenei's death and his son's succession, Iran has intensified attacks on US and Israeli targets, expanding the conflict to the UAE and Bahrain.  Hezbollah has also joined the fray, escalating regional instability and impacting global trade.

title: 'For our son, death is a release’: Harish Rana's father thanks SC for passive euthanasia ruling
content: After a 13-year struggle, the Supreme Court granted permission for the withdrawal of life-sustaining treatment for Harish Rana, who has been in a vegetative state since a 2013 accident. His parents, facing immense emotional and financial strain, expressed gratitude for the humane judgment, viewing it as a release for their son from prolonged suffering.

title: Lucknow double murder: Man rapes, kills woman, strangles partially blind son who tried to save her
content: Accused Kishan Rawat, arrested after a police encounter in Lucknow, is linked to the March 9 double murder of a woman and her partially blind son in Sisendi village. A policeman was injured during the exchange of fire. Preliminary investigations suggest a financial dispute, with rape suspected after post-mortem findings and CCTV evidence.

title: 'Had frequent disputes with techie wife': IAF wing commander dies by suicide at home
content: An Indian Air Force wing commander, Vipul Yadav (39), associated with anti-Naxal operations in Chhattisgarh, died by suicide at his Raipur residence on Wednesday. His domestic help discovered the body while his children were asleep in another room. Preliminary inquiries suggest possible domestic discord may have led to the incident.

title: ‘He doesn't need to say sorry’: Gambhir backs Arshdeep after Daryl Mitchell incident
content: Head coach Gautam Gambhir backed Arshdeep Singh after his on-field clash with Daryl Mitchell in the T20 World Cup final. Gambhir stated that competitive aggression is natural when representing the country and even suggested an apology wasn't necessary. He believes such incidents are amplified by social media, downplaying the controversy.

title: Men's Bubble Watch: Indiana, Texas will be sweating out Selection Sunday
content: The Hoosiers and Longhorns lost in their conference tournaments, leaving them right on the forecast cutlines.

title: U.S. averts WBC disaster, advances with Italy win
content: Team USA advanced out of pool play in the World Baseball Classic and will face Canada on Friday in the tournament's quarterfinals, averting a disastrous flameout thanks to Italy's 9-1 victory against Mexico on Wednesday night.

title: Crosby commits after nixed trade: I'm a Raider
content: Maxx Crosby made his first public comments since the Ravens backed out of a trade that would've sent him to Baltimore, writing on X on Wednesday night, "Im a Raider. I'm back."

title: WNBPA 'feeling movement' after latest CBA talks
content: Union president Nneka Ogwumike voiced optimism following back-to-back days of prolonged negotiations with the WNBA.

title: Hurley out after 11 seasons at Arizona State
content: Arizona State will not renew the contract of men's basketball coach Bobby Hurley, the school announced Wednesday, hours after it was eliminated from the Big 12 tournament in a blowout loss to Iowa State.

title: Big Ten asks NCAA to pause tampering inquiries
content: The Big Ten sent a letter to the NCAA asking the organization to suspend investigations and enforcement related to tampering, and modernize its rules.

title: Court filing: Jackson feared Pearce 'will kill me'
content: WNBA player Rickea Jackson filed a petition for protection against her ex-boyfriend, Falcons player James Pearce Jr., saying she was "in fear of my life" and, if the court didn't intervene, she believed "James will kill me," according to court documents.

title: Clark back with double-double in Team USA debut
content: Playing in her first game since July, Caitlin Clark had 17 points and 12 assists in 19 minutes to lead Team USA to a 110-64 win in its first game of the FIBA World Cup qualifiers.

title: Carroll predicts QB Smith will rebound with Jets
content: Pete Carroll, in his first interview since being fired by the Raiders, took the blame for Geno Smith's struggles in Las Vegas.

title: 🏆 NFL free agency awards: Best and worst moves
content: The edge rush market exploded, the Titans called on old friends, Mike Evans landed in a new place, and two players were very particular about their contract numbers.

title: Live tracking NFL free agency: Updates on signings, trades, cuts
content: We're following NFL free agency signings, trades, cuts, contract restructures and other moves as they happen.

title: Barnwell makes sense of Maxx Crosby trade, Ravens' ensuing Trey Hendrickson signing
content: Maxx Crosby was traded to the Ravens, the trade was called off and Baltimore signed Trey Hendrickson.

title: Women's college basketball awards and All-Americans: UConn's Sarah Strong leads way
content: Mikayla Blakes made sure POY wasn't a one-player race, and Aaliyah Chavez had company in the FOY talk.

title: Three takeaways from Team USA's win, Caitlin Clark's debut
content: Six players, including Clark, made their debuts for the U.S. national team. How did they look in USA's win?

title: The Ravens' pivot from Maxx Crosby to Trey Hendrickson: Answering four big questions
content: By moving on from Crosby, Baltimore was still able to land a four-time Pro Bowl pass rusher in Hendrickson.

title: Power Rankings: Thunder, Spurs rule the West, Celtics rise
content: The NBA playoffs are near. Which teams are best positioned for the home stretch of the regular season?

title: World Baseball Classic tiebreaker scenarios: How can Team USA advance?
content: The United States' WBC fate is now out of its hands. Here's how the U.S. can move on -- or be sent packing.

title: Men's basketball coaching carousel tracker: Who could be up next at Arizona State?
content: The Sun Devils are moving on from Bobby Hurley after 11 seasons -- the sixth power-conference job to open.

title: 30 MLB teams, 30 breakout stars: The players who will make the biggest leap in 2026
content: From veterans in new roles to former top prospects ready to reach new heights, here's who we expect to level up.

title: UCL talking points: Spurs simply awful as all Premier League teams fail to win
content: ESPN writers Mark Ogden, Sam Tighe, Gab Marcotti and Alex Kirkland break down the action as it happened, reflect on the first legs, and look ahead to next week's return fixtures.

title: Blank in Nashville puts Miami's Concacaf Champions Cup dream on hold
content: Co-owner Jorge Mas has made it clear: Inter Miami wants to win the Concacaf Champions Cup. And the pressure is on Leo Messi and Co. after a drab 0-0 first-leg draw in the round of 16 on Wednesday.

title: Avalanche now unbeatable? East contenders whiffed big-time? Judging NHL trade deadline overreactions
content: The final week resulted in a last flurry of trades -- and many hot takes. We identify which takeaways are legit and which are way off base.

title: Playoff watch: Projecting the Eastern wild-card teams
content: Montreal, Ottawa, Washington and Philadelphia will play on Wednesday. Here are playoff projections and more.

title: All the records Bam Adebayo set in his stunning 83-point(!) night
content: Heat big man Bam Adebayo just put up the second-highest scoring game in NBA history, with 83 points in a win over the Wizards.

title: On the rise? Teams, players poised to improve in 2026
content: From potential CFP contenders to bounce-back candidates, we've got good feelings about these programs.

title: Can Brooks Koepka contend? Who are some sleeper picks?
content: From Scottie Scheffler's attempt at a third Players title to how the course is set up to play, we have five big topics to keep an eye on this week.

title: WNBA CBA negotiations: Talks in 'right direction' after marathon meeting
content: League personnel and WNBPA staff met for nearly 12 hours Tuesday into Wednesday but did not reach a deal on a new collective bargaining agreement.

title: Latest mini NFL mock draft: Yates projects picks 1-10 based on the free agency frenzy
content: How has NFL free agency affected 2026 draft plans? Here are Field Yates' updated predictions for picks Nos. 1-10 after the first wave of deals.

title: DT Hargrave stays in NFC North, joins Packers
content: Defensive tackle Javon Hargrave reached agreement Wednesday on a two-year, $23 million contract with the Packers, agent Drew Rosenhaus told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Sources: Ravens pivot, agree to add Hendrickson
content: The Ravens have agreed to sign Trey Hendrickson, sources told ESPN, in a high-priced pivot after their canceled blockbuster trade for Maxx Crosby.

title: Sources: Byard, NFL's INTs leader in '25, to Pats
content: Free agent safety Kevin Byard III has agreed to sign with the Patriots on a one-year, $9 million deal, according to NFL Network.

title: Colts, QB Jones agree to 2-year, $88M contract
content: The Colts and quarterback Daniel Jones reached agreement Wednesday on a two-year, $88 million contract that can be worth up to $100 million, his agents told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Sources: Pats land Packers' Doubs on 4-year deal
content: The Patriots have agreed to sign former Packers wide receiver Romeo Doubs to a four-year deal worth up to $80 million, sources told ESPN.

title: Sources: TE Knox stays with Bills on new deal
content: The Bills found a way to keep tight end Dawson Knox on the 2026 roster amid a difficult cap situation by signing him to a new three-year contract, sources told ESPN's Jeremy Fowler on Tuesday.

title: Texans bolster O-line, reach deal with OT Smith
content: Free agent offensive tackle Braden Smith reached agreement with the Houston Texans on a two-year, $25 million contract that includes $13.5 million fully guaranteed, his agent told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Source: WR Raymond leaves Lions to join Bears
content: Kalif Raymond has agreed to sign a one-year contract with the Bears, reuniting the wide receiver with Ben Johnson, a source told ESPN's Jeremy Fowler.

title: Tatum's return vaults Celtics, SGA adds distance in MVP race
content: The Celtics are now +550 to win the NBA championship, while Shai Gilgeous-Alexander is distancing himself in MVP race.

title: Ravens tied for second-shortest Super Bowl odds after whirlwind week
content: Every NFL team's team's win total, as well as odds to win their division, the conference and Super Bowl LXI.

title: Is Iran in or out of World Cup? What is Iraq's status for playoffs in Mexico?
content: The ongoing conflict in Iran and neighboring countries has put their participation in the 2026 World Cup in doubt, while Iraq's ability to get to the intercontinental playoff is also in doubt. Here's the latest we know.

title: NWSL ambition rankings: Which teams are raising standards, and which aren't trying enough?
content: The NWSL has teams that are building stadiums, signing stars and spending money. Meanwhile, other teams, well, aren't doing any of that.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal join Man City in race for Newcastle defender
content: Arsenal are considering an offer to sign Newcastle right back Tino Livramento. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Kings of Command: Nine fantasy breakout pitchers to draft late
content: Which overlooked pitchers have the skills to come up with a big season? Tristan H. Cockcroft presents his annual look at MLB's "Kings of Command."

title: Eric Karabell's 'Do not draft' list
content: Karabell identifies the players unlikely to be on his roster this season due to too much risk, too much hype.

title: Guide to NFL offseason signings: Can Geno Smith rebound from 2025 in New York?
content: Your one-stop shop for fantasy football reaction to all the key player movement via free agency and trades.

title: 'Insult to national flag': Complaint filed against Hardik Pandya over T20 WC celebration
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most." Read More

title: Held captive, 21 Nagas released in Manipur’s restive Ukhrul district
content: The bodies of two Kuki men were recovered from a jungle a day after they went missing when “Naga volunteers” reportedly opened fire

title: ‘Fuel stocks sufficient in T.N.’: CM Stalin urges calm as motorists crowd outlets
content: Retail fuel outlets across the city, as well as in other cities and towns in the State, have been witnessing a sudden spike in customer volume since Wednesday afternoon

title: Prepare plans for ring roads around Telangana dist HQs, study connecting them to NHs: Telangana CM
content: Telangana CM says land will be allotted for bus terminal near proposed high-speed rail corridor, wants only EV vehicles for official purposes

title: MP urges inclusion of Mysuru in ‘urban economic zone’ scheme
content: The scheme is aimed at improving modern infrastructure in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities with a population of more than five lakh, as well as in major pilgrimage centres

title: Kumbh Mela fame Monalisa Bhosle defends interfaith marriage with actor Farman Khan in Kerala, rejects ‘love jihad’ claims
content: Monalisa Bhosle says the decision to marry Farman Khan was entirely her own and that she had insisted on the marriage despite initial hesitation from him

title: Is YSRCP treading a tough road?
content: Decimated from holding 151 seats in the Andhra Pradesh Assembly to just 11 in the 2024 Assembly elections, the YSRCP now appears to be fighting its way back to relevance as the leading Opposition force

title: Watch: Kharge and Nadda spar in Parliament over Farooq Abdullah attack
content: Tensions flared in the Rajya Sabha after an alleged attempt on former Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Farooq Abdullah’s life. Opposition leader Mallikarjun Kharge questioned the government on security lapses, while Union Minister J.P. Nadda assured a full investigation.

title: Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar assumes additional charge as Tamil Nadu Governor
content: Hailing from Goa, Mr. Arlekar assumed office as Governor of Kerala in January 2025. Prior to that, he served as Governor in Himachal Pradesh and then as Governor of Bihar.

title: LPG shortage: Power generation in Karnataka may be hit by natural gas supply priority list issued by Centre
content: The Centre recently issued a gazette notification under the Natural Gas (Supply Regulation) Order, 2026, prioritising sectors for gas allocation as the country faces supply constraints triggered by the US–Israel–Iran conflict in West Asia

title: Kerala veteran CPI(M) leader G. Sudhakaran to contest as Independent candidate from Ambalappuzha in Assembly polls
content: Sudhakaran, however, says he has ‘no plans of abandoning the Communist party’s ideals, to which he remains steadfast to’, or to join any other party or front

title: Andhra CM Chandrababu Naidu lauds Nellore Collector Himanshu Shukla’s initiative
content: Himanshu Shukla’s brain child ‘One Month-One Village-Four Visits’ programme has resolved the land-related issues in 26 villages so far

title: Opposition did not adhere to rules, forcing me to make difficult decisions: Om Birla in Lok Sabha
content: Om Birla, speaking in the Lok Sabha on the no-confidence motion moved against him, said the Chair does not belong to one person and that he will uphold constitutional dignity in the Parliament

title: The Iran war and the uncertain future of the expats
content: The Gulf will continue to matter for India, but the old certainty of the “Gulf dream” has been shaken

title: YSRCP stands for values, credibility: Former Andhra CM Jagan Mohan Reddy
content: Jagan was addressing YSRCP leaders on the occasion of the party’s Formation Day celebrations at its central office in Tadepalli in Guntur district

title: CPR training for 400 Indian Army personnel in Bengaluru on March 13
content: The session will be conducted by the International Critical Air Transfer Team (ICATT) Foundation in association with Society for Emergency Medicine in India (SEMI)

title: LPG shortage: Samridhi@Kochi feels the heat as cooking gas supply dries up in Kerala
content: Samridhi’s central kitchen at Paramara Road in Ernakulam North, along with its Fort Kochi outlet and three canteens are currently reportedly operating on the last lot of 30-odd commercial LPG cylinders arranged by the Corporation as an emergency measure

title: Palaniswami, Vijay slam DMK government over women’s safety in T.N.
content: In a post on X, Mr. Palaniswami said, “Sexual violence against women and the lack of their safety have become routine in Tamil Nadu.”

title: Kerala HC orders removal of Vellappally Natesan, other SNDP Yogam office-bearers from their posts
content: Order follows findings by Department of Registration that they had acted in violation of Companies Law by failing to file audited accounts of yogam for three consecutive years from 2023

title: R.N. Ravi takes oath as 22nd Governor of West Bengal
content: R.N. Ravi was appointed the Governor of West Bengal following the resignation of his predecessor C.V. Ananda Bose on March 5

title: Samriddhi Yatra: Nitish Kumar launches projects worth ₹781 crore
content: Bihar Deputy Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary who was also present, stressed that the Nitish Kumar-led NDA government in Bihar is working without any discrimination, with the motto of “Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas”

title: Opposition MPs protest in Parliament complex over shortage of commercial LPG cylinders
content: Rahul Gandhi alleged that the Prime Minister is panicked because of the Epstein files issue and the Adani Case in the U.S.

title: Heatwave in many areas in western States, above normal temperatures in north: IMD
content: Gujarat experienced heatwave to severe heatwave conditions; Ahmedabad and Gandhinagar saw 7.2 degrees Celsius and 6.4 degrees Celsius above normal maximum temperatures

title: Watch: West Asia crisis exposes risks to India’s oil security and policy gaps | The Hindu Editorial
content: The escalating crisis in West Asia has raised fresh concerns over India’s energy security as the country imports nearly 90% of its oil. With the Strait of Hormuz remaining a critical supply route, disruptions could have major economic consequences.

title: Sri Lankan Navy arrests two Indian fishermen near Katchatheevu
content: The fishermen from Rameswaram were taken to the naval port in Talaimannar for further interrogation, Fisheries Department officials here said

title: Hyderabad police arrests two for manufacturing adulterated ginger paste in unhygienic conditions
content: The accused allegedly prepared ginger paste in unhygienic conditions using spoiled raw material and chemicals

title: 26 injured as fire breaks out at electric meter factory in Noida
content: The injured were among nearly 240 workers who were trapped inside the factory, Capital Power System Limited, in the Phase 1 police station area when the fire broke out

title: Belagavi to get flights to Mumbai, Navi Mumbai and Indore in summer schedule
content: IndiGo will add capacity and increase frequency on the Belagavi–Hyderabad sector as part of the summer schedule

title: Curfew relaxed in Meghalaya's East Garo Hills for five hours on March 12
content: East Garo Hills Deputy Commissioner R P Marak said the relaxation was allowed to enable residents to procure essential commodities, while security forces continued to maintain vigil in sensitive areas.

title: Civil rights groups demand Maharashtra’s anti-conversion bill draft be made public
content: Activists question why the Bill is being brought about at a time when similar legislations have been challenged in the apex court

title: Attempts on to control LPG crisis, says Union Minister Suresh Gopi
content: The Union Minister of State for Petroleum and Natural Gas says ‘due to diplomatic sensitivities, some matters related to supply through Hormuz Strait cannot be revealed’

title: Police identify 2,900 mule bank accounts in Belagavi used for cyber fraud
content: Cybercriminals are convincing unemployed youth and women to either open new accounts or share details of their existing bank account, in exchange for a commission

title: Revision in schedule of DEMU trains between Alnavar and Dandeli
content: South Western Railway has revised the days of operation and timetable of DEMU trains running between Alnavar and Dandeli, and the changes will come into effect on March 17

title: Parliament Budget session LIVE: Lok Sabha debates Supplementary Demand for Grants; Rajya Sabha discusses Rural Development Ministry
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge on Wednesday demanded that Prime Minister Narendra Modi respond in Parliament to the emerging economic impact of the conflict in West Asia

title: Kadapa court slaps 20-year RI on rape convict, tells State to pay compensation to victim
content: B.S. Mohammad Basha (41) of Sugamanchipalli in Jammalamadugu mandal of Kadapa district was accused of hoodwinking a minor girl with a false promise of marriage

title: Transit pass must now for vehicles entering Bihar with minerals
content: The decision has been taken by Deputy Chief Minister Vijay Kumar Sinha, who also holds the Mines and Geology department; he said that it will make the mineral transportation system more transparent, lawful and revenue-oriented in the state.

title: India cosponsors UNSC resolution condemning ‘egregious’ attacks by Iran against GCC nations, Jordan
content: The 15-nation Security Council, currently under the presidency of the United States, adopted the resolution with 13 votes in favour, none against and abstentions by veto-wielding permanent members China and Russia

title: Over ₹5.63 crore penalty imposed on periodicals, 88,315 publications cancelled: I&B Ministry

content: The Indian Kayaking & Canoeing Association (IKCA) on February 3, 2026, wrote to J&K Sports Minister Satish Sharma to spare “Ms. Bilquis Mir as coach for the national team preparation for the upcoming Asian Games 2026”.

content: Scientists For People raises concerns over air and marine pollution, livelihoods of local people and health hazards

title: 25-qubit quantum computing system installed at IIIT-Dharwad, Raichur
content: QpiAI-Indus is a 25-qubit, superconducting quantum computer developed by Bengaluru-based startup QpiAI in April 2025 as part of India’s National Quantum Mission

title: Congress criticises BJP for gas shortages, highlighting public suffering amid viral videos of long queues in Uttar Pradesh
content: “A woman collapsing and fainting in line reveals the complete breakdown of the government’s distribution system, and the value of public life is now a pittance,” said Ajay Rai, President, Uttar Pradesh Congress unit.

title: Groups to prevent human-elephant conflict linked to more elephant deaths
content: Anti-depredation squads were formed in villages to incentivise communities to join hands with the Forest Department to respond to elephants together. However, the searchlights and firecrackers the squads used to drive elephants away possibly created a ‘landscape of fear’, forcing elephants to stray into more dangerous situations

title: Madhya Pradesh govt directs Collectors to review LPG supply arrangements, prevent black marketing
content: In a letter dated March 10, the govt has asked the collectors to regularly review the stock availability as well as suggest potential alternatives to commercial consumers

content: Although revenue spending on science and technology has risen in recent years, State funding for fundamental research has been modest

title: India joins 30 UN Member States to express deep alarm at escalation of hostilities in Lebanon

title: CDSCO issues advisory to prevent misleading promotion of prescription drugs for obesity
content: Activities that exaggerate therapeutic claims, guarantee weight-loss outcomes, or undermine the importance of lifestyle modifications such as diet and physical activity will be treated as misleading promotion

title: Sales of electric cooktops sizzle as LPG flameout worries families
